```json
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 971 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 51. Record Vote Number: 82.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 971 proposed by Senator Duckworth. (consideration: CR S1112-1113) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting access to fertility services, and eliminating barriers for families in need of high-quality, affordable fertility services by expanding nationwide coverage for in vitro fertilization.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",

                  "text": "Amendment SA 957 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 84.",

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",

                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 957 proposed by Senator Lujan. (consideration: CR S1115-1116) To strike reconciliation instructions requiring damaging cuts to programs critical to rural Americans and food assistance for American families.",

        },
        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 925 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T02:39:30Z",
                      "rollNumber": "67",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00067.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 925 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 67.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 925.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 925 proposed by Senator Hickenlooper. (consideration: CR S1103) To create a point of order against legislation that would raise energy costs for Americans, including higher monthly electricity bills, building material expenses, and transportation costs.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 925 proposed by Senator Hickenlooper.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T02:39:30Z",
                      "rollNumber": "67",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00067.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 925 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 67.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "8"
          },

            "actionDate": "2025-02-20",

              "link": {
                "name": "SA 925",

              }
            },
            "text": "Amendment SA 925 ruled out of order by the chair."
          },
          "number": "925",
          "onBehalfOfSponsor": {
            "item": {
              "bioguideId": "H000273",
              "firstName": "John",
              "fullName": "Sen. Hickenlooper, John W. [D-CO]",
              "lastName": "Hickenlooper",
              "party": "D",
              "state": "CO",
              "type": "Proposed on behalf of"
            }
          },
          "proposedDate": "2025-02-20T05:00:00Z",
          "purpose": "To create a point of order against legislation that would raise energy costs for Americans, including higher monthly electricity bills, building material expenses, and transportation costs.",

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T09:19:39Z",
                      "rollNumber": "85",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00085.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 922 agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 53 - 47. Record Vote Number: 85.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 922 proposed by Senator Lee. (consideration: CR S1117) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to Congress continuing its work to rein in the administrative state by supporting legislation that prevents Federal agencies from finalizing major rules without congressional approval, strengthens the Article 1 law-making powers of Congress, cuts spending resulting from costly regulations, reduces inflation, and unleashes economic growth.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",

                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 922 proposed by Senator Lee.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {

                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },

                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 911 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 911 proposed by Senator Markey. (consideration: CR S1114) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to increasing funding for research on Alzheimer's disease and related dementias.",

              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 880 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 880 proposed by Senator Murray. (consideration: CR S1118) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to reversing the Trump Administration's indiscriminate cut to biomedical research and the lifesaving work supported by the National Institutes of Health at research institutions across the country.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 880 proposed by Senator Murray.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "95000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment not agreed to: Amendment SA 880 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "6"
          },

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T02:14:22Z",
                      "rollNumber": "66",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00066.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 878 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 66.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 878 proposed by Senator Murray. (consideration: CR S1102) To strike the reconciliation instructions and create a reserve fund to implement a bipartisan, multi-year agreement to provide up to $171,000,000,000 in discretionary funding for defense and up to $171,000,000,000 in discretionary funding for other programs, accounts, and activities to address border, veterans, farmers, food and nutrition, disaster relief, and other needs.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 878 proposed by Senator Murray.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",

                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 776 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 51. Record Vote Number: 71.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 776.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 776 proposed by Senator Schumer. (consideration: CR S1105-1106) To prevent tax cuts for the wealthy if a single dollar of Medicaid funding is cut.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 776 proposed by Senator Schumer.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {

                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {

                      "rollNumber": "81",

                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 699 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 81.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 699 proposed by Senator Lujan. (consideration: CR S1112) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to supporting police, which may include initiatives that provide funding directly to law enforcement agencies to hire or rehire additional career law enforcement officers in an effort to increase their community policing capacity and crime prevention efforts.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 699 proposed by Senator Lujan.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {

                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 659 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 52. Record Vote Number: 83.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 659 proposed by Senator Blumenthal. (consideration: CR S1113-1114) To ensure full and uninterrupted funding for Department of Veterans Affairs health care and benefits provided by the Sergeant First Class Heath Robinson Honoring our Promise to Address Comprehensive Toxics Act of 2022 (Public Law 117-168), also known as the \"PACT Act\", preventing any cuts or delays.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",

                  "sourceSystem": {

                  "text": "Amendment SA 540 proposed by Senator Bennet.             "text": "Amendment SA 540 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 68."

          "proposedDate": "2025-02-20T05:00:00Z",

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 494 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T00:58:15Z",
                      "rollNumber": "63",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00063.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 494 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 63.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 494.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 494 proposed by Senator Klobuchar. (consideration: CR S1100) To stop tax cuts for the ultra-rich while families struggle to put food on the table.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 494 proposed by Senator Klobuchar.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T00:58:15Z",
                      "rollNumber": "63",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",

                    }

                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 494 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 63.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "8"

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 454 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T00:35:07Z",
                      "rollNumber": "62",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00062.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 454 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 52. Record Vote Number: 62.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 454.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 454 proposed by Senator Schumer. (consideration: CR S1099-1100) To prevent unwarranted tax cuts for the ultra-rich.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 454 proposed by Senator Schumer.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T00:35:07Z",
                      "rollNumber": "62",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00062.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 454 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 52. Record Vote Number: 62.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "8"
          },

        },
        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T07:14:52Z",
                      "rollNumber": "80",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00080.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 436 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 80.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 436 proposed by Senator Shaheen. (consideration: CR S1111-1112) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preserving and extending vital tax credits enacted under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, which make heath care accessible and affordable and that have led to the lowest uninsured rate in our Nation's history.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {

                  },

                },

                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 436 proposed by Senator Shaheen.",
                  "type": "Floor"

                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 407 proposed by Senator Ossoff. (consideration: CR S1106) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting access to maternal and pediatric health care through Medicaid.",

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 311 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 311 proposed by Senator Blunt Rochester. (consideration: CR S1117-1118) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preventing the indiscriminate termination of Federal employees who protect the health or safety of Americans, which may include scientists, emergency preparedness staff, frontline health care workers, drug or medical device reviewers, or other employees at the Department of Health and Human Services.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 311 proposed by Senator Blunt Rochester.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "95000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment not agreed to: Amendment SA 311 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "6"
          },

            "text": "Amendment SA 299 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 76."

          "number": "299",
          "onBehalfOfSponsor": {

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 276 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T05:10:17Z",
                      "rollNumber": "74",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00074.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 276 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 74.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 276.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 276 proposed by Senator Baldwin. (consideration: CR S1107-1108) To create a point of order against legislation that would take away health care from seniors, including those receiving care in nursing homes, through cuts to the Medicaid program.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 276 proposed by Senator Baldwin.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T05:10:17Z",
                      "rollNumber": "74",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00074.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 276 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 48 - 52. Record Vote Number: 74.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "8"
          },

                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 233 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 51. Record Vote Number: 79.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 233.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 233 proposed by Senator Van Hollen. (consideration: CR S1110-1111) To create a point of order against legislation that would cut funding from the school lunch or school breakfast programs.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 172 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T05:33:06Z",
                      "rollNumber": "75",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00075.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 172 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 51. Record Vote Number: 75.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 172.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 172 proposed by Senator Reed. (consideration: CR S1108) To create a point of order against legislation that would reduce Medicare and Medicaid benefits for Americans.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 172 proposed by Senator Reed.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T05:33:06Z",
                      "rollNumber": "75",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00075.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 172 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 51. Record Vote Number: 75.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "8"
          },
          "amendedBill": {
            "congress": "119",
            "number": "7",

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 130 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T01:49:18Z",
                      "rollNumber": "65",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00065.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 130 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 65.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 130.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 130 proposed by Senator Warner. (consideration: CR S1101-1102) To create a point of order against any reconciliation bill that would not decrease the cost of housing for American families.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-19",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 130 proposed by Senator Warner.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T01:49:18Z",
                      "rollNumber": "65",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00065.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 130 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 65.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-19",

            "count": "8"

                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1223 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",

                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1223 proposed by Senator Coons. (consideration: CR S1114-1115) To protect Americans' privacy from unauthorized access by DOGE.",

                  "text": "Amendment SA 1207 not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 49 - 51. Record Vote Number: 86.",

                    "code": "0",

                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1207 proposed by Senator Merkley. (consideration: CR S1118-1119) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to ending price gouging on prescription drugs.",

        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1156 ruled out of order by the chair.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T04:49:59Z",
                      "rollNumber": "73",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00073.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 1156 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 73.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Point of order that the amendment violates section 305(b)(2) of the CBA raised in Senate with respect to amendment SA 1156.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1156 proposed by Senator Wyden. (consideration: CR S1106-1107) To prevent millions of Americans from being kicked off their health coverage, suffering needlessly, getting sicker, and dying sooner.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 1156 proposed by Senator Wyden.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "91000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment submitted",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "97000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-20",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-02-21T04:49:59Z",
                      "rollNumber": "73",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00073.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Roll call votes on amendments in Senate: Motion to waive all applicable budgetary discipline with respect to amendment SA 1156 rejected in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 73.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                }
              ]
            },
            "count": "8"
          },

              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 101 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 101 proposed by Senator Heinrich. (consideration: CR S1113) To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to funding for grants awarded by the Office on Violence Against Women.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-19",
                  "sourceSystem": {

                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment proposed (on the floor): Amendment SA 101 proposed by Senator Heinrich.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },

                  "actionCode": "95000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-02-21",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "text": "Senate amendment not agreed to: Amendment SA 101 not agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },

                  "sourceSystem": {
```

## API Data: fullTexts

```json
{
  "fullTexts": [
    {
      "fetchError": null,
      "sourceFormat": "Formatted XML",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/sconres/BILLS-119sconres7rs.xml",
      "text": "II\nCalendar No. 13\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nS. CON. RES. 7\nIN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES\nFebruary 13, 2025 Mr. Graham , from the Committee on the Budget , reported the following original concurrent resolution; which was placed on the calendar\nCONCURRENT RESOLUTION\nSetting forth the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2025 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.\n#### 1. Concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025\n##### (a) Declaration\nCongress declares that this resolution is the concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025 and that this resolution sets forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.\n##### (b) Table of contents\nThe table of contents for this concurrent resolution is as follows:\nSec. 1. Concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025.\nTITLE I\u2014Recommended levels and amounts\nSubtitle A\u2014Budgetary levels in both Houses\nSec. 1101. Recommended levels and amounts.\nSec. 1102. Major functional categories.\nSubtitle B\u2014Levels and amounts in the Senate\nSec. 1201. Social Security in the Senate.\nSec. 1202. Postal Service discretionary administrative expenses in the Senate.\nTITLE II\u2014Reconciliation\nSec. 2001. Reconciliation in the House of Representatives.\nSec. 2002. Reconciliation in the Senate.\nTITLE III\u2014Reserve funds\nSec. 3001. Reserve fund for reconciliation legislation.\nSec. 3002. Reserve fund for deficit-neutral legislation.\nTITLE IV\u2014Other matters\nSec. 4001. Enforcement filing.\nSec. 4002. Budgetary treatment of administrative expenses.\nSec. 4003. Application and effect of changes in allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels.\nSec. 4004. Adjustment authority for revisions to statutory caps.\nSec. 4005. Adjustments to reflect changes in concepts and definitions.\nSec. 4006. Adjustment for changes in the baseline.\nSec. 4007. Exercise of rulemaking powers.\nI\nRecommended levels and amounts\nA\nBudgetary levels in both Houses\n#### 1101. Recommended levels and amounts\nThe following budgetary levels are appropriate for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2034:\n**(1) Federal revenues**\nFor purposes of the enforcement of this resolution:\n**(A)**\nThe recommended levels of Federal revenues are as follows:\nFiscal year 2025: $3,853,053,000,000.\nFiscal year 2026: $4,005,633,000,000.\nFiscal year 2027: $4,095,208,000,000.\nFiscal year 2028: $4,221,709,000,000.\nFiscal year 2029: $4,343,708,000,000.\nFiscal year 2030: $4,536,585,000,000.\nFiscal year 2031: $4,744,851,000,000.\nFiscal year 2032: $4,939,252,000,000.\nFiscal year 2033: $5,155,399,000,000.\nFiscal year 2034: $5,375,311,000,000.\n**(B)**\nThe amounts by which the aggregate levels of Federal revenues should be changed are as follows:\nFiscal year 2025: \u2212$5,916,000,000.\nFiscal year 2026: \u2212$211,035,000,000.\nFiscal year 2027: \u2212$421,185,000,000.\nFiscal year 2028: \u2212$415,138,000,000.\nFiscal year 2029: \u2212$416,123,000,000.\nFiscal year 2030: \u2212$422,056,000,000.\nFiscal year 2031: \u2212$435,419,000,000.\nFiscal year 2032: \u2212$449,460,000,000.\nFiscal year 2033: \u2212$467,244,000,000.\nFiscal year 2034: \u2212$484,719,000,000.\n**(2) Federal revenue changes relative to current policy**\nThe amounts by which the aggregate levels of Federal revenues should be Reconciliation in the House of Representatives\n##### (a) Committee on Agriculture\nThe Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (b) Committee on Armed Services\nThe Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $150,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (c) Committee on Education and Workforce\nThe Committee on Education and Workforce of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (d) Committee on Energy and Commerce\nThe Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (e) Committee on Natural Resources\nThe Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (f) Committee on Homeland Security\nThe Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $175,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (g) Committee on the Judiciary\nThe Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $175,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (h) Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure\nThe Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $20,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (i) Submissions\nIn the House of Representatives, not later than March 7, 2025, the committees named in the subsections of this section shall submit their recommendations to the Committee on the Budget of the House of Representatives to carry out this section.\n#### 2002. Reconciliation in the Senate\n##### (a) Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry\nThe Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (b) Committee on Armed Services\nThe Committee on Armed Services of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $150,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (c) Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation\nThe Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $20,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (d) Committee on Energy and Natural Resources\nThe Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (e) Committee on Environment and Public Works\nThe Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (f) Committee on Finance\nThe Committee on Finance of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (g) Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions\nThe Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that reduce the deficit by not less than $1,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (h) Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs\nThe Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $175,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (i) Committee on the Judiciary\nThe Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate shall report changes in laws within its jurisdiction that increase the deficit by not more than $175,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (j) Submissions\nIn the Senate, not later than March 7, 2025, the committees named in the subsections of this section shall submit their recommendations to the Committee on the Budget of the Senate. Upon receiving all such recommendations, the Committee on the Budget of the Senate shall report to the Senate a reconciliation bill carrying out all such recommendations without any substantive revision.\nIII\nReserve funds\n#### 3001. Reserve fund for reconciliation legislation\n##### (a) House of Representatives\n**(1) In general**\nIn the House of the Representatives, the chair of the Committee on the Budget may revise the allocations of a committee or committees, aggregates, and other appropriate levels in this resolution for any bill or joint resolution considered pursuant to section 2001 containing the recommendations of one or more committees, or for one or more amendments to, a conference report on, or an amendment between the Houses in relation to such a bill or joint resolution, by the amounts necessary to accommodate the budgetary effects of the legislation, if the budgetary effects of the legislation comply with the reconciliation instructions under this concurrent resolution.\n**(2) Determination of compliance**\nFor purposes of this section, compliance with the reconciliation instructions under this concurrent resolution shall be determined by the chair of the Committee on the Budget of the House of Representatives.\n**(3) Exception for legislation**\nThe point of order set forth in clause 10 of rule XXI of the House of Representatives shall not apply to reconciliation legislation reported by the Committee on the Budget pursuant to submissions under section 2001.\n##### (b) Senate\n**(1) In general**\nIn the Senate, the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate may revise the allocations of a committee or committees, aggregates, and other appropriate levels in this resolution, and make adjustments to the pay-as-you-go ledger, for any bill or joint resolution considered pursuant to section 2002 containing the recommendations of one or more committees, or for one or more amendments to, a conference report on, or an amendment between the Houses in relation to such a bill or joint resolution, by the amounts necessary to accommodate the budgetary effects of the legislation, if the budgetary effects of the legislation comply with the reconciliation instructions under this concurrent resolution.\n**(2) Determination of compliance**\nFor purposes of this section, compliance with the reconciliation instructions under this concurrent resolution shall be determined by the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate.\n**(3) Exceptions for legislation**\n**(A) Short-term**\nSection 404 of S. Con. Res. 13 (111th Congress), the concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2010, as amended by section 3201(b)(2) of S. Con. Res. 11 (114th Congress), the concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2016, shall not apply to legislation for which the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate has exercised the authority under paragraph (1).\n**(B) Long-term**\nSection 3101 of S. Con. Res. 11 (114th Congress), the concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2016, shall not apply to legislation for which the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate has exercised the authority under paragraph (1).\n#### 3002. Reserve fund for deficit-neutral legislation\n##### (a) Senate\nThe Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate may revise the allocations of a committee or committees, aggregates, and other appropriate levels in this resolution, and make adjustments to the pay-as-you-go ledger, for one or more bills, joint resolutions, amendments, amendments between the Houses, motions, or conference reports by the amounts provided in such legislation, provided that such legislation would not increase the deficit over the period of the total of fiscal years 2025 through 2034.\n##### (b) House of Representatives\nThe chair of the Committee on the Budget of the House of Representatives may revise the allocations of a committee or committees, aggregates, and other appropriate levels in this concurrent resolution for one or more bills, joint resolutions, amendments, or conference reports by the amounts provided in such legislation, provided that such legislation would not increase the deficit for the period of fiscal year 2025 to fiscal year 2034.\nIV\nOther matters\n#### 4001. Enforcement filing\n##### (a) In the House of Representatives\nIn the House of Representatives, if a concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025 is adopted without the appointment of a committee of conference on the disagreeing votes of the two Houses with respect to this concurrent resolution on the budget, for the purpose of enforcing the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 621 et seq. ) and applicable rules and requirements set forth in the concurrent resolution on the budget, the allocations provided for in this subsection shall apply in the House of Representatives in the same manner as if such allocations were in a joint explanatory statement accompanying a conference report on the budget for fiscal year 2025. The chair of the Committee on the Budget of the House of Representatives shall submit a statement for publication in the Congressional Record containing\u2014\n**(1)**\nfor the Committee on Appropriations, committee allocations for fiscal year 2025 consistent with title I for the purpose of enforcing section 302 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633 ); and\n**(2)**\nfor all committees other than the Committee on Appropriations, committee allocations consistent with title I for fiscal year 2025 and for the period of fiscal years 2025 through 2034 for the purpose of enforcing 302 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633 ).\n##### (b) In the Senate\nIf this concurrent resolution on the budget is agreed to by the Senate and House of Representatives without the appointment of a committee of conference on the disagreeing votes of the two Houses, the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate may submit a statement for publication in the Congressional Record containing\u2014\n**(1)**\nfor the Committee on Appropriations, committee allocations for fiscal year 2025 consistent with the levels in title I for the purpose of enforcing section 302 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633 ); and\n**(2)**\nfor all committees other than the Committee on Appropriations, committee allocations for fiscal years 2025, 2025 through 2029, and 2025 through 2034 consistent with the levels in title I for the purpose of enforcing section 302 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633 ).\n#### 4002. Budgetary treatment of administrative expenses\n##### (a) Senate\n**(1) In general**\nIn the Senate, notwithstanding section 302(a)(1) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(a)(1) ), section 13301 of the Budget Enforcement Act of 1990 ( 2 U.S.C. 632 note), and section 2009a of title 39, United States Code, the report or the joint explanatory statement accompanying this concurrent resolution on the budget or the statement filed pursuant to section 4001(b), as applicable, shall include in an allocation under section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(a) ) to the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate of amounts for the discretionary administrative expenses of the Social Security Administration and the United States Postal Service.\n**(2) Special rule**\nIn the Senate, for purposes of enforcing section 302(f) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(f) ), estimates of the level of total new budget authority and total outlays provided by a measure shall include any discretionary amounts described in paragraph (1).\n##### (b) House of Representatives\n**(1) In general**\nIn the House of Representatives, notwithstanding section 302(a)(1) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(a)(1) ), section 13301 of the Budget Enforcement Act of 1990 ( 2 U.S.C. 632 note), and section 2009a of title 39, United States Code, the report or the joint explanatory statement accompanying this concurrent resolution on the budget or the statement filed pursuant to section 4001(a), as applicable, shall include in an allocation under section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(a) ) to the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives of amounts for the discretionary administrative expenses of the Social Security Administration and the United States Postal Service.\n**(2) Special rule**\nIn the House of Representatives, for purposes of enforcing section 302(f) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(f) ), estimates of the level of total new budget authority and total outlays provided by a measure shall include any discretionary amounts described in paragraph (1).\n#### 4003. Application and effect of changes in allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels\n##### (a) Application\nAny adjustments of allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels made pursuant to this concurrent resolution shall\u2014\n**(1)**\napply while that measure is under consideration;\n**(2)**\ntake effect upon the enactment of that measure; and\n**(3)**\nbe published in the Congressional Record as soon as practicable.\n##### (b) Effect of changed allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels\nRevised allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels resulting from these adjustments shall be considered for the purposes of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 621 et seq. ) as the allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels contained in this concurrent resolution.\n##### (c) Budget committee determinations\nFor purposes of this concurrent resolution, the levels of new budget authority, outlays, direct spending, new entitlement authority, revenues, deficits, and surpluses for a fiscal year or period of fiscal years shall be determined on the basis of estimates made by the chair of the Committee on the Budget of the applicable House of Congress.\n#### 4004. Adjustment authority for revisions to statutory caps\nDuring the 119th Congress, if a legislative measure is enacted that revises the discretionary spending limit established under subsection (c) of section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 ( 2 U.S.C. 901 ), the Chair of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate may, consistent with the legislative measure and as necessary\u2014\n**(1)**\nadjust the allocation required under section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 ( 2 U.S.C. 633(a) ) to the appropriate committee or committees of the Senate; and\n**(2)**\nadjust all other budgetary aggregates, allocations, levels, and limits established under this Concurrent Resolution.\n#### 4005. Adjustments to reflect changes in concepts and definitions\n##### (a) House of Representatives\nIn the House of Representatives, the chair of the Committee on the Budget may adjust the appropriate aggregates, allocations, and other budgetary levels in this concurrent resolution for any change in budgetary concepts and definitions consistent with section 251(b)(1) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 ( 2 U.S.C. 901(b)(1) ).\n##### (b) Senate\nIn the Senate, upon the enactment of a bill or joint resolution providing for a change in concepts or definitions, the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate may make adjustments to the levels and allocations in this concurrent resolution in accordance with section 251(b) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 ( 2 U.S.C. 901(b) ).\n#### 4006. Adjustment for changes in the baseline\nThe chair of the Committee on the Budget of the House of Representatives and the Chairman of the Committee on the Budget of the Senate may adjust the allocations, aggregates, and other appropriate budgetary levels in this concurrent resolution to reflect changes resulting from the Congressional Budget Office\u2019s updates to its baseline for fiscal years 2025 through 2034, including the effects of legislation enacted before the date on which this concurrent resolution is agreed to.\n#### 4007. Exercise of rulemaking powers\nCongress adopts the provisions of this title\u2014\n**(1)**\nas an exercise of the rulemaking power of the Senate and the House of Representatives, respectively, and as such they shall be considered as part of the rules of each House or of that House to which they specifically apply, and such rules shall supersede other rules only to the extent that they are inconsistent with such other rules; and\n**(2)**\nwith full recognition of the constitutional right of either the Senate or the House of Representatives to change those rules (insofar as they relate to that House) at any time, in the same manner, and to the same extent as is the case of any other rule of the Senate or House of Representatives.",
      "versionDate": "2025-02-13",
      "versionType": "RS"
    },

      "text": "119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nS. CON. RES. 7\nIN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES\nCONCURRENT RESOLUTION\nSetting forth the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2025 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.\n#### 1. Concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025\n##### (a) Declaration\nCongress declares that this resolution is the concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025 and that this resolution sets forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.\n##### (b) Table of contents\nThe table of contents for this concurrent resolution is as follows:\nSec. 1. Concurrent resolution on the budget for fiscal year 2025.\nTITLE I\u2014Recommended levels and amounts\nSubtitle A\u2014Budgetary levels in both Houses\nSec. 1101. Recommended levels and amounts.\nSec. 1102. Major functional categories.\nSubtitle B\u2014Levels and amounts in the Senate\nSec. 1201. Social Security in the Senate.\nSec. 1202. Postal Service discretionary administrative expenses in the Senate.\nTITLE II\u2014Reconciliation\nSec. 2001. Reconciliation in the House of Representatives.\nSec. 2002. Reconciliation in the Senate.\nTITLE III\u2014Reserve funds\nSec. 3001. Reserve fund for reconciliation legislation.\nSec. 3002. Reserve fund for deficit-neutral legislation.\nSec, 3003. Deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting Medicare and Medicaid.\nSec. 3004. Deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to Government deregulation.\nTITLE IV\u2014Other matters\nSec. 4001. Enforcement filing.\nSec. 4002. Budgetary treatment of administrative expenses.\nSec. 4003. Application and effect of changes in allocations, aggregates, and other budgetary levels.\nSec. 4004. Adjustment authority for revisions to statutory caps.\nSec. 4005. Adjustments to reflect changes in concepts and definitions.\nSec. 4006. Adjustment for changes in the baseline.\nSec. 4007. Exercise of rulemaking powers.\nI\nRecommended levels and amounts\nA\nBudgetary levels in both Houses\n#### 1101. Recommended levels and amounts\nThe following budgetary levels are appropriate for each of fiscal years 2025 through 2034:\n**(1) Federal revenues**\nFor purposes of the enforcement of this
```
